images.alt: A beautiful Mediterranean couture supermodel stands in a poised elongated fashion pose inside a luminous futuristic sanctuary filled with suspended orbital mechanisms, floating glass ecosystems, and intricate celestial machinery woven into elegant white architecture. Massive concentric metallic rings rotate through the air while hanging terrariums and reflective pools create layered spatial depth beneath cool diffused daylight. She wears an avant-garde haute couture gown inspired by crystalline marine anatomy and precision-engineered geometry, featuring translucent icy-blue pleated panels, intricate silver lattice embroidery, sculptural wing-like structures, delicate mesh corsetry, and flowing layered organza that moves like liquid glass around her silhouette. Her textured dark hair is styled in a loose editorial updo with calm, emotionally distant body language and graceful anti-gravity posture. Hyper-detailed luxury fashion photography, surreal sci-fi worldbuilding, cool silver and blue palette, atmospheric softness, impossible architecture, cinematic editorial storytelling, ultra-realistic textures, shallow depth of field, portrait orientation.

images.alt:
A long, symmetrical washroom stretches into the distance beneath harsh fluorescent ceiling lights, its damp tiled floor reflecting pale green and amber tones like standing water after a storm. Rows of old porcelain sinks line both sides of the room beneath cracked mirrors and exposed pipes, while numbered shower stalls stand partially open along the walls. Steam hangs thick in the center aisle, obscuring visibility and turning the far end of the room into a glowing white void.
Two motionless figures stand halfway down the corridor, one on each side, dressed in long pale coats that appear wet or plasticized under the lights. Their faces are hidden as they hold sheets of paper loosely at their sides, positioned like silent attendants or observers waiting for something inevitable to arrive.
From the dense fog at the center doorway, a ghostly human silhouette slowly emerges. The figure is nearly consumed by vapor, appearing translucent and unfinished, with only faint legs and shoulders visible through the haze. The overhead lights cast cold reflections across the slick floor while warmer wall lamps add a sickly institutional glow. Moisture glistens on the tiles and pipes, amplifying the oppressive humidity of the room. The entire scene feels suspended between hospital, prison shower, and nightmare, as if this corridor exists outside ordinary time and only reveals itself briefly through steam and fluorescent hum.
Keywords:
liminal horror, steam-filled corridor, institutional washroom, ghostly silhouette, fluorescent lighting, tiled hallway, symmetrical composition, psychological dread, wet reflections, silent attendants, cinematic fog, abandoned facility

images.alt:
A towering dragon-like creature fills nearly the entire frame in a dramatic close-up portrait against a sky consumed by smoke and fire. Its massive head is angled slightly downward toward the viewer, creating an intimidating sense of scale and presence. The creature’s skin appears rough and weathered, layered with cracked scales in deep cobalt blue, ash gray, and bruised crimson tones. Large ivory horns curve upward from its skull at uneven angles, some chipped and scarred as though worn down by centuries of battle. Jagged black and blue spines erupt from the back of its head and neck like shattered obsidian blades.
The dragon’s most striking feature is its enormous eye, glowing pale gold with a narrow red slit pupil fixed directly at the viewer. The eye conveys both animal awareness and unsettling intelligence. Around the eye, irritated red flesh contrasts sharply against the cold blue scales. Its muzzle is broad and skeletal, with oversized fangs protruding irregularly from cracked jaws that resemble fossilized bone. Additional fin-like crimson spikes flare outward near the cheek and neck area, adding to the creature’s alien silhouette.
Behind the dragon, thick clouds swirl in violent layers of charcoal gray and burning orange, suggesting an active inferno or collapsing volcanic landscape. The lighting is warm and smoky, reflecting off the scales and horns with a painterly glow. The overall composition feels like a cinematic fantasy illustration capturing a colossal beast moments before unleashing destruction.
Keywords:
dragon, apocalypse, horns, scales, monster, fantasy creature, glowing eye, fire clouds, cinematic portrait, jagged spines, dark fantasy, colossal beast

record.text: personally I think it's a very big deal that the Canada bureau chief for the @nytimes.com — certainly one of the highest-paid journalists in the country — asked an unspecified "AI tool" what Poilievre said & published its AI-hallucinated quotes in her reporting.

images.alt:
Editors’ Note: May 1, 2026
This article was updated after The Times learned that a remark attributed to Pierre Poilievre, the Conservative leader, was in fact an A.I.-generated summary of his views about Canadian politics that A.I. rendered as a quotation. The reporter should have checked the accuracy of what the A.I. tool returned.  The article now accurately quotes from a speech delivered by Mr. Poilievre in April. He said, “My personal opinion is that when a member of Parliament goes back on the word they made to their constituents and switches parties, constituents should be able to petition to throw them out and have a byelection. That would put the people back in charge of our democracy rather than having dirty backroom Liberal deals by Mark Carney determine our destiny.” He did not refer to politicians who changed allegiances as turncoats in that speech.

The word generative is sixty-one years old. Max Bense coined it on 5 February 1965 in Stuttgart, in an essay describing how programs could compute aesthetics. 
I guess Bense didn't claim: "this changes everything..."
#GenerativeAI #AI

Everyone talks about Generative UI like it was invented in 2026.

Muriel Cooper built Information Landscapes at MIT in 1985: three-dimensional, dynamic typography you could fly through. She called it a "new medium" before anyone took interface seriously. Four decades early.

Star Trek, 1987. Actress terrified of the bridge panels. Designer: "Every button you press is the right button. The system adapts to you." Adaptive interfaces, 30 years before your phone learnt how you type. Read more on my Substack:

h1manu.substack.com/p/what-jony-ive-borrowed-from-star

Nous Research 提出了 Lighthouse Attention，一個針對長 context 訓練的 Attention 機制。它在訓練階段能提升 1.4 到 1.7 倍的預訓練速度，而且訓練完就可以移除，不影響 Inference 階段。Lighthouse Attention 透過多解析度金字塔，對 Q, K, V 進行對稱 pooling，將 Attention 運算從 O(N·S·d) 降到 O(S²·d)。實測在 98K context 的 Llama-3-style model 上，速度有顯著提升，同時保持或降低了訓練損失。這對於需要處理長 context 的模型訓練來

Poetiq 的 Meta-System 展示了一個有趣的實測結果。他們自動建構了一個模型無關（model-agnostic）的推論框架（inference harness），用來優化 LiveCodeBench Pro 的表現。過程中只使用了 Gemini 3.1 Pro，沒有進行任何模型微調（fine-tuning）。

實測結果是，這個框架在多個不同的 LLM 上都帶來了性能提升，包括 GPT 5.5 High、Kimi K2.6 和 Gemini 3.0 Flash 等，且都無需針對各別模型做修改。這顯示了在不改變模型本身的前提下，透過優化推論流程來提升 LLM 表現的潛力。

#LL

LLM Multi-Agent 系統在處理複雜任務時，協作能力受到廣泛關注。但實測上，任務失敗仍是常見情況，而且通常還伴隨大量無效動作。PSU 和 Duke 的研究人員探討了如何自動歸因這些失敗，找出是哪個 agent 在什麼時間點導致了問題。這對於理解 Multi-Agent 系統的瓶頸，並進一步優化系統表現，目前看來是個關鍵方向。

#LLM #MultiAgent #FailureAttribution

The setting is a sun-baked, endless desert highway at twilight—straight out of a gritty Film Noir—but the desert is dotted with strange, white flowers that glow softly, giving it that eerie Midsommar fertility cult energy. I am standing in the middle of the road, wearing a tattered Victorian dress that’s been modified with brass gears, leather straps, and steam vents for that Steampunk edge. My hair is loose and blowing in the hot wind, partially obscuring my face. The texture is grainy and textured, like old film stock, with dust scratches vignetting the corners. My expression is hauntingly blank, staring off into the distance where the road meets the void. The vibe is desolate, atmospheric, and totally unnerving.
